name: okta_login_from_blocked_country
description: A user successfully signed in from a country on the org's high-risk/blocked list (user.session.start with geo.country in a configured deny-list).

validation.notes: Honeycomb-native detection - there is no Panther Okta equivalent (Panther uses geo-velocity / 'New Geo-Location' behavior heuristics rather than a fixed country list). TEMPLATE: replace the geo.country deny-list with the org's actual high-risk/sanctioned countries before deploying. The default list (North Korea, Iran, Syria, Cuba, Russia, Belarus) is an illustrative OFAC-style starting point only - confirm each value against the exact geo.country display strings emitted in this dataset, because the field uses full English display names (observed: 'United States', 'United Kingdom', 'The Netherlands', 'Hong Kong') and the spelling/format for the blocked countries cannot be validated here (zero traffic from them in 90d). geo.country values are populated and the 'in' operator was validated against live low-volume countries (Egypt/Thailand/Singapore). LIMITATIONS: country granularity only - sanctioned sub-national regions (e.g. Crimea, Donetsk, Luhansk) resolve to their parent country and are not separable; VPN/proxy egress can mask or fake the apparent country (pair with okta_anonymizing_vpn_login). Filtering to okta.outcome.result = SUCCESS targets completed logins; drop that filter to also catch blocked-country attempts.
